content.text: The Hope United Activity Tracking System helps you record and report on daily activities and attendee participation. The system supports both individual check-ins and group event tracking, with comprehensive reporting capabilities.
When you open the application, you'll see the home page with these options:

text: - Register new participants in the system

content.text: For administrative functions, navigate directly to
How to Register a New Participant

ol: From the home page, click "Open Registration Form"
Scan the participant's QR code or enter their information manually
Complete all required fields including demographics and waiver agreement
The participant is now in the system and can log activities

content.text: Note: Registration is a one-time process. Once registered, participants can check in for activities using their QR code or name.

ol: Click "Open Activity Tracking" from the home page
Scan your QR code or enter your name to log in
Select one or more activity categories that apply to your visit

content.text: For staff recording activities on behalf of participants:

ol: Navigate to /admin and click "Manual Activity Entry"
Select the date of the activity
Search for and select the participant by name
Choose one or more activity categories

text: - This is where you record group counts

content.text: When recording activities for group events (e.g., a Social Event with 50 people):

text: field allows you to specify how many people attended

ul: For a group event with 50 people, enter "50" in the attendee count field
The system will count this as 50 people in all reports and totals
You can still track individual participants by name while also recording anonymous group attendance

content.text: Important: The attendee count ensures your reports show the total number of people who participated, not just the number of activity records created.
The system tracks the following activity categories:
Participants can select multiple categories per visit if they participate in multiple activities.
Access the admin dashboard at /admin. Available functions:

text: - View monthly activity summaries by category
- Generate detailed reports for specific participants or date ranges
- Record activities on behalf of participants
- Launch a demo session for testing

ul: Monthly totals for each activity category
Grand totals by category and month
Year-over-year comparisons (switch years using the year picker)
Detailed daily breakdowns (expand each month to see day-by-day counts)

content.text: All totals reflect the actual number of people who attended based on the attendee count entered for each activity.
This report provides detailed information about individual participants:

ul: Filter by date range, name, ZIP code, or demographics
View detailed activity history with timestamps

text: - Total attendance count (sum of all attendee counts)

content.text: Click "Download XLSX" on the Activity Report page to export a spreadsheet with:

ul: Formatted for easy viewing and analysis in Excel

text.text: Always enter the correct number of attendees

text: when recording group activities. This ensures accurate people counts in reports.

ol: Record activities on the day they occur when possible
Use the manual entry feature for backdating activities if needed
Select all applicable categories for each visit
Review reports regularly to ensure data accuracy

ul: Count all attendees before entering the activity
Choose the most relevant category (e.g., "Social Event" for community gatherings)
Enter the total count in the "Number of Attendees" field
You can optionally record individual named participants and still add anonymous group counts
Always log out of the admin dashboard when finished
Respect participant privacy when viewing reports
Use anonymous group counts when individual names are not needed

content.text: Q: What's the difference between Total Visits and Total Unique Person Visits?

A: Total Visits counts the sum of all attendee counts entered (so a group event with 50 people counts as 50 visits). Total Unique Person Visits counts unique combinations of person and day (so if someone attends multiple activities on the same day, it only counts once).
Q: How do I record an event where I don't have names for all attendees?
A: Use the manual activity entry and increase the "Number of Attendees" field to reflect the total count. The system will track the total number of people without requiring individual names.
Q: Can I edit or delete activities after they're entered?
A: Currently, activities cannot be edited or deleted through the web interface. Contact your system administrator if you need to make corrections.
Q: What happens if I forget to log someone's activity?
A: Use the Manual Activity Entry function in the admin dashboard. Select the correct date, find the participant, choose the categories, and save.
Q: How far back can I view activity data?
A: The system stores all historical data. Use the year picker in the Activity Report to view different years, or use date range filters in the Activity by Person report.
For technical support or questions about using the system, contact your system administrator or IT support team.
